data.body: CI BLOCKER (R6 #760 + R7 #761 both Verified-1, recurring): flaky CONCURRENCY tests fail in CI but PASS locally on this exact base (d5ec73bc) under same parallel settings. #760 x2: test_enrich_queue_prune_never_drops_concurrent_locked_writes (integration tier; assertion 'lost=30 swallowed_write_raises=30' = real write-loss race under CI contention, plausibly aggravated by identity-rollout signing centralization) + test_benchmark_commit_count_is_ceil_per_pass (ValueError int('')). #761: test_code_review_emit_retains_the_cap (default suite py3.13; git 'invalid object/Error building trees' during commit-under-lock). ALL unrelated to my docs/plan-review changes. Rechecked both once more (probabilistic — flakes are CI-load-triggered). If persists, this is a MAIN-side concurrency fragility to surface to operator, not a fault of R6/R7. Meanwhile advancing experiment plans (no CI dep).
